mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in retail sales signals stronger household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth has Rank IC below 0.03 for 20-day S&P 500 forward returns over any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or have average returns no lower than non-triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate does not rise, real policy accommodation falls and inflation-risk premia compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rises while FEDFUNDS is unchanged or lower over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL acceleration and flat or falling FEDFUNDS has non-negative coefficient for 20-day returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production signals weaker cyclical output and margin pressure in goods-producing sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contractions are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or show no negative mean-return differential.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate stronger construction activity, credit transmission, and durable-goods demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 6-month moving average crosses above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST uptrend triggers have average 60-day S&P 500 returns no higher than the unconditional average in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate tightens discount rates and financial conditions, lowering equity valuation support and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day S&P 500 returns after FEDFUNDS tightening triggers are positive in more than 60% of cases or have non-negative average abnormal returns.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate signals broad income growth and durable consumption support, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive and UNRATE falls over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-falling trigger does not outperform the unconditional 20-day S&P 500 return hit rate by at least 5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production weakens despite positive real GDP growth, the equity market prices a late-cycle divergence between headline demand and producer profits, producing negative 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth is negative while latest GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is positive
failure_condition: Reject if this INDPRO-GDPC1 divergence trigger has non-negative mean 60-day S&P 500 forward returns or a negative-signal hit rate below 50%.
